blurb: Manages Alberta's fiscal framework, administers provincial taxes and levies, oversees pensions, insurance, and financial regulation, manages unclaimed property, and regulates gaming, liquor, and cannabis — ensuring sound fiscal management and consumer protection.

goals.title: OUTCOME 1: Alberta has a fair and efficient tax system that funds public services
goals.description: The ministry administers Alberta's provincial tax system including corporate income tax, personal income tax credits and benefits, fuel tax, tobacco tax, tourism levy, and other levies through Tax and Revenue Administration (TRA).

foundational_services.name: Administer provincial tax policy and revenue collection
foundational_services.desc: Develops tax policy, administers tax legislation, collects provincial taxes and levies, and manages compliance and enforcement programs through Tax and Revenue Administration.

main_services.name: File and pay provincial taxes and levies

supporting.name: File and pay Alberta corporate income tax
supporting.desc: Corporations operating in Alberta file corporate income tax returns and pay provincial corporate income tax through Tax and Revenue Administration. Alberta's corporate tax rate is among the lowest in Canada.

relationships.service: Access the provincial budget and fiscal reports

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of corporate filers
reach_estimate.basis: All corporations operating in Alberta must file provincial corporate income tax

economic_impact.note: Corporate income tax is a major provincial revenue source funding public services

supporting.name: File and pay fuel tax, tobacco tax, or tourism levy
supporting.desc: Businesses that sell fuel, tobacco products, or provide tourism accommodation file returns and remit applicable levies to TRA. Includes fuel tax, tobacco tax, tourism levy, and the 911 levy.
supporting.users: Fuel retailers, tobacco distributors, accommodation providers

reach_estimate.basis: Fuel tax and tobacco tax apply to all retailers and distributors in the province

economic_impact.note: Levies fund transportation infrastructure, health programs, and emergency services

main_services.name: Access tax credits, benefits, and exemptions

supporting.name: Check your eligibility for the Alberta Child and Family Benefit
supporting.desc: Low- and middle-income families with children may be eligible for the Alberta Child and Family Benefit, a non-taxable quarterly payment. The benefit is calculated automatically based on federal income tax returns.

channels.label: Alberta Child and Family Benefit information

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Alberta families
reach_estimate.basis: The ACFB supports families with adjusted net income below approximately $43,000

economic_impact.note: Direct income support reduces child poverty and supports family financial stability

supporting.name: Apply for an Alberta Indian Tax Exemption
supporting.desc: First Nations individuals with a Certificate of Indian Status can claim exemption from provincial fuel tax and tobacco tax on qualifying purchases made on-reserve or delivered to a reserve.
supporting.users: First Nations individuals with status cards

channels.label: Participating retailers on or near reserves

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of eligible individuals
reach_estimate.basis: Alberta has over 100,000 registered First Nations members

economic_impact.note: Tax exemption upholds treaty rights and federal Indian Act provisions

goals.title: OUTCOME 2: Albertans' pensions and insurance are secure and well-regulated
goals.description: The ministry regulates pensions, insurance, and financial services to protect Albertans' retirement savings and ensure insurance markets are fair and stable. Includes the Superintendent of Insurance, pension oversight, and securities regulation.

foundational_services.name: Regulate pensions, insurance, and financial services in Alberta
foundational_services.desc: Oversees the registration and regulation of pension plans, licenses insurance companies and adjusters, supervises securities markets through the Alberta Securities Commission, and develops regulatory policy to protect consumers and ensure market stability.
foundational_services.users: Pension plan administrators, insurance companies, securities firms, government regulators

main_services.name: Access pension information or file a pension complaint

supporting.name: Find information about your employer pension plan
supporting.desc: Albertans enrolled in a registered pension plan can access information about pension plan registration, member rights, plan governance, and what happens to your pension when you change jobs or retire. The Superintendent of Pensions oversees plan compliance.
supporting.users: Pension plan members, retirees, employers with pension plans

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of pension plan members
reach_estimate.basis: Over 800 registered pension plans in Alberta covering hundreds of thousands of members

economic_impact.note: Pension oversight protects Albertans' retirement savings and plan integrity

supporting.name: File a complaint about a pension plan
supporting.desc: If you believe your employer pension plan is not being administered properly — such as missing contributions, incorrect benefit calculations, or governance concerns — you can file a complaint with the Superintendent of Pensions for investigation.

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of complaints and inquiries annually
reach_estimate.basis: The Superintendent investigates pension plan compliance issues

economic_impact.note: Complaint investigation deters pension plan mismanagement and protects members

main_services.name: Get insurance information or file an insurance complaint

supporting.name: Find information about insurance regulation and your rights
supporting.desc: Albertans can access information about insurance regulation, policyholder rights, auto insurance, home insurance, and how insurance companies are supervised. The Superintendent of Insurance regulates the industry to ensure fair treatment of consumers.
supporting.users: Insurance policyholders, consumers shopping for insurance

reach_estimate.estimate: Virtually all Alberta adults hold insurance policies
reach_estimate.basis: Auto insurance is mandatory; home, life, and health insurance are widespread

economic_impact.note: Insurance regulation protects consumers and ensures market stability

goals.title: OUTCOME 3: Alberta's finances are managed transparently and public resources are well-stewarded
goals.description: The ministry manages Alberta's fiscal framework, publishes the provincial budget, regulates gaming, liquor, and cannabis, administers unclaimed property, and ensures accountability and transparency in public finances.

foundational_services.name: Develop the provincial fiscal framework and budget
foundational_services.desc: Develops the provincial budget, fiscal plan, and economic outlook. Manages the Heritage Savings Trust Fund and other fiscal reserves. Provides economic analysis and fiscal forecasting to support government decision-making.

main_services.name: Access gaming, liquor, or cannabis licences and information

supporting.name: Apply for or renew a gaming, liquor, or cannabis licence
supporting.desc: Businesses and non-profits can apply for licences to sell liquor, operate gaming activities (casinos, VLTs, raffles, bingos), or sell cannabis. The Alberta Gaming, Liquor and Cannabis Commission (AGLC) administers licensing, compliance, and enforcement.
supporting.users: Restaurant and bar owners, gaming operators, cannabis retailers, non-profits running raffles

reach_estimate.estimate: Tens of thousands of licensed establishments and gaming/raffle operators
reach_estimate.basis: AGLC licenses thousands of liquor, gaming, and cannabis establishments across Alberta

economic_impact.note: Gaming and liquor licensing supports the hospitality industry and generates significant provincial revenue

supporting.name: Report a concern about a licensed establishment
supporting.desc: Albertans can report concerns about a licensed liquor establishment, gaming operator, or cannabis retailer to AGLC for investigation. This includes concerns about overcrowding, underage sales, intoxication, or licence violations.
supporting.users: Members of the public, community organizations

reach_estimate.estimate: Thousands of complaints and inspections annually
reach_estimate.basis: AGLC conducts compliance inspections and investigates complaints province-wide

economic_impact.note: Compliance enforcement protects public safety and maintains industry standards

main_services.name: Claim unclaimed property or access fiscal information

supporting.name: Search for and claim unclaimed property
supporting.desc: Albertans can search a registry of unclaimed personal property — including forgotten bank accounts, insurance benefits, investments, and other financial assets — and submit a claim to recover their property. Over $168 million in unclaimed property is held by the province.
supporting.users: All Albertans, estates and heirs of deceased Albertans

legislation.full_name: Unclaimed Personal Property and Vested Property Act

reach_estimate.estimate: Over 340,000 unclaimed property items worth $168 million
reach_estimate.basis: Large and growing registry of unclaimed assets with broad public interest

economic_impact.note: Returning unclaimed property to rightful owners strengthens financial security

supporting.name: Access the provincial budget and fiscal reports
supporting.desc: Albertans can access the provincial budget, quarterly fiscal updates, the Heritage Savings Trust Fund report, government financial statements, and the economic outlook to understand how public funds are managed and allocated.

relationships.service: File and pay Alberta corporate income tax

reach_estimate.estimate: Hundreds of thousands of Albertans access budget information
reach_estimate.basis: The provincial budget affects all Albertans and receives broad public and media attention

economic_impact.note: Fiscal transparency builds public confidence in government financial management
